reasoning: MUST continue to fix critical memory exhaustion blocking Q3/Q6 execution. Q1 shows promise (271ms, only 1.36x slower than DuckDB), but 2/3 queries crash with std::bad_alloc due to loading all 16 lineitem columns (6GB). Implementing lazy column loading + dictionary encoding + right-sized hash tables (priorities 1-3) will reduce memory from 15.7GB to ~6-8GB, enabling all queries to run. These are low-risk, high-impact changes. One iteration remains for execution-time optimizations after memory is fixed.
focus_areas: Lazy column loading with projection pushdown (CRITICAL - load only 4-7 columns instead of 16 for lineitem)
Dictionary encoding for low-cardinality strings (c_mktsegment, o_orderpriority, o_orderstatus)
Right-size Q3 hash table reservations (reduce qualifying_orders from 1M to 350K)
strategy_notes: Focus exclusively on MEMORY REDUCTION this iteration. Do NOT attempt SIMD optimizations yet. Goal: get all 3 queries to execute successfully by dropping peak memory from 15.7GB to ~6-8GB. Priority order: (1) Lazy column loading is MANDATORY and must be implemented first - this alone fixes the crash. (2) Dictionary encoding is secondary but high-value (memory + speed). (3) Hash table sizing is tertiary optimization. Test memory usage after each change. Save execution-time optimizations (SIMD filters, vectorized aggregation) for iteration 3 after confirming queries run.
notes: Implementation guidance: For recommendation 0, modify storage/storage.h to add optional std::vector<std::string> columns parameter to load_lineitem(), load_customer(), load_orders(). In storage.cpp, skip unwanted columns during parsing. Update query files to request only needed columns: Q1 needs {l_shipdate, l_quantity, l_extendedprice, l_discount, l_tax, l_returnflag, l_linestatus}, Q3 needs {l_orderkey, l_shipdate, l_extendedprice, l_discount}, Q6 needs {l_shipdate, l_quantity, l_discount, l_extendedprice}. For recommendation 1, create storage/string_dict.h with StringDictionary class. For recommendation 2, change reserve() calls in queries/q3.cpp. Expected outcome: All queries execute, Q1 time stays ~271ms, Q3 runs in ~400-2000ms, Q6 runs in ~100-400ms. Memory drops below 8GB.
